response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of broad engineering experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, CI infrastructure, IDE components, MCP code generators), but this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows no people-management experience at all. His profile fits a senior/staff IC or tech-lead track far better than an engineering manager role focused on team development, performance management, and organizational roadmaps.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, well beyond the 8-year minimum

Real developer-tooling and testing-infrastructure work: test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD and GitLab infrastructure, IDE components at National Instruments, and recent AI developer tooling
Demonstrated teaching and mentoring inclination through workshops, multi-day courses, and community talks
concerns: No people-management experience, missing the hard 2-year minimum for a management role centered on 1:1s, performance expectations, and team development
No evidence of formal technical leadership of teams, strategic roadmap ownership, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform experience is limited and dated (iOS/Apple TV work in 2015-2016), a weak match for a role supporting a major mobile platform ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure background
Depth and breadth of engineering experience
